chain.thought: Explanation: In 2008, the throws were 71.00 m and 71.40 m, with a maximum of 71.40 m. In 2009, the throws were 72.85 m, 74.79 m, and 70.45 m, with a maximum of 74.79 m. Since 74.79 m is greater than 71.40 m, the athlete threw more meters in 2009. To compare which year had the greater throw, you only need to maximize the distance; minimizing is not necessary.

judge_thought: Explanation: The table lists throws for 2008 (71.00 m and 71.40 m) and for 2009 (72.85 m, 74.79 m, and 70.45 m). Whether comparing the best single throw (74.79 m vs. 71.40 m) or the total for the year (218.09 m vs. 142.40 m), 2009 has greater distances than 2008. As for whether to both maximize and minimize the distance, you cannot simultaneously maximize and minimize the same metric, so the correct response is no.
